description_long: This object is a classic, early-generation mobile phone, often referred to as a 'brick phone.' It features a tall, rectangular body with a protruding, black antenna at the top. The front face displays a numeric keypad with large, tactile buttons, a small monochrome screen above the keypad, and a speaker grille at the very top. The casing is primarily a light beige or off-white plastic, with a slightly textured, matte finish. The sides and back are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the utilitarian design typical of mobile phones from the 1980s. The bottom front bezel includes a visible brand label and model information, adding to its retro aesthetic.
description: A retro, beige brick-style mobile phone with a prominent antenna, large keypad, and small monochrome screen. The design is utilitarian and iconic of early mobile technology.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keypad, small screen, speaker grille, and brand label at the bottom.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the phone's thick, rectangular profile and the protruding antenna.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain, unadorned back and the antenna's attachment point.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the phone's thickness and smooth surface.
attribute_brand_logo: A brand label is present at the bottom front bezel, featuring text and possibly a small logo, printed in black and red on a white background.
attribute_screen_display_content: The screen is blank, with no visible content, icons, or interface elements displayed.
attribute_camera_placement: None; there are no visible camera modules or lenses on any side of the object.
attribute_button_layout: The front face features a numeric keypad with 12 large rectangular buttons arranged in a grid, along with several smaller function buttons below the screen. No side or rear buttons are visible.
attribute_case_or_cover_design: None; the object does not have any external case, cover, or protective accessory.
